docs.text: autosar-data API Documentation API documenation for the autosar-data Python library AutosarModel The class AutosarModel represents the Autosar data model. It contains all files and elements. Constructor AutosarModel() Create a new AutosarModel Functions create_file def create_file( self, filename: str, version: AutosarVersion = AutosarVersion.LATEST ) -> ArxmlFile Create a new file in the model load_buffer def load_buffer( self, buffer: str, filename: str, strict: bool = False ) -> Tuple[ArxmlFile, List[str]] Load a buffer (string) as arxml load_file def load_file(self, filename: str, strict: bool = False) -> Tuple[ArxmlFile, List[str]] Load an arxml file remove_file def remove_file(self, arxmlfile: ArxmlFile) -> None Remove a file from the model. Any elements belonging exclusively to that file will also be removed. serialize_files def serialize_files(self) -> Dict[str, str] Serialize all files individually, to generate a dict(filename, serialized content), write def write(self) -> None Write all files in the model to disk get_element_by_path def get_element_by_path(self, autosar_path: str) -> Element Get an identifiable element in the model by its Autosar path sort def sort(self) -> None Sort the entire model in place. Takes all ordering constraints into account. get_references_to def get_references_to(self, target_path: str) -> List[Element] Get all reference elements which refer to the given Autosar path check_references def check_references(self) -> List[Element] Check all references in the model and return a list of elements containing invalid references Class members files files: List[ArxmlFile] A list of ArxmlFile objects containing all files in the model root_element root_element: Element The root element of the model, <AUTOSAR> elements_dfs elements_dfs: ElementsDfsIterator Depth first dearch iterator over all elements in the model, regardless of their association with a file identifiable_elements identifiable_elements: List[str] List of all paths of identifiable elements in the model ArxmlFile Represents a file that is part of an AutosarModel Functions serialize def serialize(self) -> str Serialize the the file to a string. This string can be loaded as valid arxml if is written to disk. check_version_compatibility def check_version_compatibility(self, version: AutosarVersion) -> List[IncompatibleItemError] check if the elements in the file would be compatible with the given target version. Returns a list of compatibility errors, each of which is an IncompatibleElementError, IncompatibleAttributeError or IncompatibleAttributeValueError Class members filename filename: str filename of the arxml file. Must be unique within the model. version version: AutosarVersion Autosar version of the file model model: AutosarModel the autosar data model which this file is part of elements_dfs elements_dfs: ArxmlFileElementsDfsIterator dfs iterator over all elements in this file xml_standalone xml_standalone: bool contains the xml standalone attribute (if any) in the xml file header Element An element in the Autosar data model Functions create_sub_element def create_sub_element( self, element_name: ElementName, position: int = None ) -> Element create a sub element under this element with the given ElementName (optionally at a specific position) create_named_sub_element def create_named_sub_element( self, element_name: ElementName, item_name: str, position: int = None ) -> Element create a named sub element under this element with the given ElementName (optionally at a specific position) create_copied_sub_element def create_copied_sub_element( self, other: Element, position: int = None ) -> Element create a copy of some other element (with all of its children) as a child of this element (optionally at a specific position) move_element_here def move_element_here( self, move_element: Element, position: int = None ) -> Element move an element from somewhere else in this model or from another model to become a child element (optionally at a specific position) remove_sub_element def remove_sub_element(self, element: Element) -> None remove a sub element and all of its content get_sub_element def get_sub_element(self, name_str: str) -> Element get a sub element by its element name. If there are several then this returns the first of them get_sub_element_at def get_sub_element_at(self, position: int) -> Element get an element by its position among the content of this element get_named_sub_element def get_named_sub_element(self, item_name: str) -> Element: Get the sub element with the given item name, if any get_bsw_sub_element def get_bsw_sub_element(self, definition_ref: str) -> Element: get the sub element with the given definition ref. It is possible to specify either the full definition ref, or only the last part after the final '/' serialize def serialize(self) -> str serialize this element and its sub elements int oa string. This string is valid xml, but it is not a vaild arxml file remove_character_data def remove_character_data(self) -> None remove the character data insert_character_content_item def insert_character_content_item(self, chardata: str, position: int) -> None for elements with ElementType mixed, this allows character data to be inserted at any point in the content of this element remove_character_content_item def remove_character_content_item(self, position: int) -> None remove one character content item from the given position attribute_value def attribute_value(self, attrname: AttributeName) -> CharacterData get the attribute value of a specific attribute. Returns None if that attribute is not set set_attribute def set_attribute(self, attrname: AttributeName, chardata: CharacterData) -> None set the given attribute to the provided value. If the attribute is valid for this element it will be created or modified as needed. remove_attribute def remove_attribute(self, attrname: AttributeName) -> None remove an attribute from the element sort def sort(self) -> None sort this element and all of its sub elements list_valid_sub_elements def list_valid_sub_elements(self) -> List[ValidSubElementInfo] provide information about valid sub elements as a list of ValidSubElementInfo add_to_file def add_to_file(self, file: ArxmlFile) -> None add the element to a file. if necessary all parent elements of this element also become part of the file remove_from_file def remove_from_file(self, file: ArxmlFile) -> None remove this element from a file. Does not affect parent elements. When an element is no longer part of any file it is deleted. Class members parent parent: Element reference to the parent of this element element_name element_name: ElementName ElementName of this element, e.g. AUTOSAR or AR-PACKAGE element_type element_type: ElementType Reference to the element type of the element in the specification item_name item_name: str item name of an identifiable element, or None for elements which are not identifiable. Setting this value renames the element and updates all references to it. is_identifiable is_identifiable: bool true if the element is identifiable, false otherwise is_reference is_reference: bool true if the element can contain a reference to another element path path: str the full autosar path of an identifiable element model model: AutosarModel reference to the model containing this element content_type content_type: ContentType content type of the element: character data ( <X>some text</X> ), elements ( <X><Y></Y></X> ), or mixed reference_target reference_target: Element returns the target of the reference, if the element contains a reference position position: int the position of this element in the content of its parent sub_elements sub_elements: ElementsIterator an iterator over all sub elements in the content of this element. It skips character data content items elements_dfs elements_dfs: ElementsDfsIterator depth first search iterator for this element and all of its sub elements character_data character_data: CharacterData character content of this element, if any. For elements with ContentType=Element, or empty elements this is None content_item_count content_item_count: int number of content items (character data and/or sub elements) content content: ElementContentIterator iterator over all content of this element attributes attributes: AttributeIterator iterator over all attributes of this element file_membership file_membership: Tuple[bool, FrozenSet[ArxmlFile]] file membership information: the tuple (is_local, set(ArxmlFile)) tells if there is a restriction to file membership attached to this element, and which files the element is part of xml_path xml_path: str a path listing all xml elements from the root of the model to the element. This is intended for display. e.g. in error messages AutosarVersion A version of the Autosar standard Autosar_4_0_1 Autosar_4_0_2 Autosar_4_0_3 Autosar_4_1_1 Autosar_4_1_2 Autosar_4_1_3 Autosar_4_2_1 Autosar_4_2_2 Autosar_4_3_0 Autosar_00042 Autosar_00043 Autosar_00044 Autosar_00045 Autosar_00046 Autosar_00047 Autosar_00048 Autosar_00049 Autosar_00050 Autosar_00051 LATEST Alias for the latest version of the standard CharacterData CharacterData: TypeAlias = Union[EnumItem, str, int, float] ElementType Functions Type of an Element in the specification splittable_in def splittable_in(self, version: AutosarVersion) -> bool is this element splittable in a particular AutosarVersion reference_dest_value def reference_dest_value(self, target: ElementType) -> EnumItem helper to determine the correct value for the DEST attribute when setting a reference find_sub_element def find_sub_element( self, target_name: ElementName, version: VersionSpecification ) -> ElementType Find the ElementType of the named sub element in the specification of this ElementType find_attribute_spec def find_attribute_spec(self, attribute_name: AttributeName) -> AttributeSpec Find the specification for the given attribute name Class members is_named is_named: bool Elements of this type must have a SHORT-NAME is_ref is_ref: bool Elements of this type must contain an autosar path in their character data, and have a DEST attribute is_ordered is_ordered: bool Ordered elements may not be sorted, since the sub element order is semantically meaningful splittable splittable: List[AutosarVersion] A list of AutosarVersions in which this element is splittable std_restriction std_restriction: str Indicates if the element type is restricted to a particular version. std_restriction contains a string, one of 'NotRestricted', 'ClassicPlatform' or 'AdaptivePlatform'. chardata_spec chardata_spec: CharacterDataType The specification of the character data content of elements of this type attributes_spec attributes_spec: List[AttributeSpec] A list of the specifications of all attributes allowed on elements of this type IncompatibleItemError IncompatibleItemError: TypeAlias = Union[ IncompatibleAttributeError, IncompatibleAttributeValueError, IncompatibleElementError ] IncompatibleAttributeError Information about an attribute that is incompatible with a given target version Class members element element: Element Element which contains the incompatible attribute attribute attribute: AttributeName Incompatible attribute allowed_versions allowed_versions: List[AutosarVersion] list of versions in which the attribute is permitted on this element IncompatibleAttributeValueError Information about an attribute value that is incompatible with a given target version Class members element element: Element Element which contains the incompatible attribute value attribute attribute: AttributeName Attribute which contains the invalid value attribute_value attribute_value: str The incompatible attribute value allowed_versions allowed_versions: List[AutosarVersion] list of versions in which the attribute value is permitted on this attribute IncompatibleElementError Information about an element that is incompatible with a given target version Class members element element: Element incompatible element allowed_versions allowed_versions: List[AutosarVersion] list of versions in which this element is compatible ValidSubElementInfo Details about a particular sub element Class members element_name element_name: str name of the potential sub element is_named is_named: bool is the sub element named, i.e. does it need to be created with create_named_sub_element is_allowed is_allowed: bool is the sub element currently allowed, given the existing content of the element. Note that some sub elements are mutually exclusive AttributeSpec The specification of an attribute Class members attribute_name attribute_name: str name of the attribute value_spec value_spec: CharacterDataType specification of the attribute value required required: bool is the attribute required or optional Globals Functions check_file def check_file(filename: str) Check if the file contains arxml data. Returns true if an arxml file header is found and does not parse anything after it. check_buffer def check_buffer(filename: bytes) Check if the buffer contains arxml data. Returns true if an arxml file header is found and does not parse anything after it. Constants __version__ __version__: str Version of the running autosar_data module. It contains a semver string of the form 'x.y.z'

docs.title: remove_attribute

docs.title: list_valid_sub_elements

docs.text: def add_to_file(self, file: ArxmlFile) -> None add the element to a file. if necessary all parent elements of this element also become part of the file

docs.text: def remove_from_file(self, file: ArxmlFile) -> None remove this element from a file. Does not affect parent elements. When an element is no longer part of any file it is deleted.
docs.title: remove_from_file

docs.text: parent: Element reference to the parent of this element
docs.title: parent

docs.text: element_name: ElementName ElementName of this element, e.g. AUTOSAR or AR-PACKAGE
docs.title: element_name

docs.text: element_type: ElementType Reference to the element type of the element in the specification
docs.title: element_type

docs.text: item_name: str item name of an identifiable element, or None for elements which are not identifiable. Setting this value renames the element and updates all references to it.
docs.title: item_name

docs.text: is_identifiable: bool true if the element is identifiable, false otherwise
docs.title: is_identifiable

docs.text: is_reference: bool true if the element can contain a reference to another element
docs.title: is_reference

docs.text: path: str the full autosar path of an identifiable element
docs.title: path

docs.text: model: AutosarModel reference to the model containing this element
docs.title: model

docs.text: content_type: ContentType content type of the element: character data ( <X>some text</X> ), elements ( <X><Y></Y></X> ), or mixed
docs.title: content_type

docs.text: reference_target: Element returns the target of the reference, if the element contains a reference
docs.title: reference_target

docs.text: position: int the position of this element in the content of its parent
docs.title: position

docs.text: sub_elements: ElementsIterator an iterator over all sub elements in the content of this element. It skips character data content items
docs.title: sub_elements

docs.text: elements_dfs: ElementsDfsIterator depth first search iterator for this element and all of its sub elements
docs.title: elements_dfs

docs.text: character_data: CharacterData character content of this element, if any. For elements with ContentType=Element, or empty elements this is None
docs.title: character_data

docs.text: content_item_count: int number of content items (character data and/or sub elements)
docs.title: content_item_count

docs.text: content: ElementContentIterator iterator over all content of this element
docs.title: content

docs.text: attributes: AttributeIterator iterator over all attributes of this element
docs.title: attributes

docs.text: file_membership: Tuple[bool, FrozenSet[ArxmlFile]] file membership information: the tuple (is_local, set(ArxmlFile)) tells if there is a restriction to file membership attached to this element, and which files the element is part of
docs.title: file_membership

docs.text: xml_path: str a path listing all xml elements from the root of the model to the element. This is intended for display. e.g. in error messages
docs.title: xml_path

docs.text: A version of the Autosar standard Autosar_4_0_1 Autosar_4_0_2 Autosar_4_0_3 Autosar_4_1_1 Autosar_4_1_2 Autosar_4_1_3 Autosar_4_2_1 Autosar_4_2_2 Autosar_4_3_0 Autosar_00042 Autosar_00043 Autosar_00044 Autosar_00045 Autosar_00046 Autosar_00047 Autosar_00048 Autosar_00049 Autosar_00050 Autosar_00051 LATEST Alias for the latest version of the standard
docs.title: AutosarVersion

docs.title: CharacterData

docs.title: splittable_in

docs.title: find_attribute_spec

docs.title: is_named

docs.text: is_ref: bool Elements of this type must contain an autosar path in their character data, and have a DEST attribute
docs.title: is_ref

docs.text: is_ordered: bool Ordered elements may not be sorted, since the sub element order is semantically meaningful

docs.text: splittable: List[AutosarVersion] A list of AutosarVersions in which this element is splittable
docs.title: splittable

docs.title: std_restriction
